title: FFCA Rulebook v0
description: Schema for the executable rulebook that operationalizes Table 5, Table 6, and Appendix C.6 of the FFCA paper (Najafi, Luo, Liu 2025).

version.description: Semver string. Bump major on breaking schema changes.

ffca_paper_ref.description: Citation handle for the paper this rulebook is derived from.

kind.description: descriptor = labels a feature/model state (e.g., archetype assignment). diagnostic = flags a pathology or recommends action.

scope.description: per_feature = evaluated for each feature. model_wide = single boolean from the static report. dynamic = needs a checkpoint sequence.

severity.description: Required for kind=diagnostic. Ignored for kind=descriptor.

diagnosis.description: Templated human-readable explanation. Can reference {feature.name}, {model.*}, etc.

recommendation.description: Actionable next step for the user.

evidence_template.description: What the agent should surface to justify the finding.

confidence_factors.description: Caveats that should lower reported confidence (e.g., 'low if n_features<10').

paper_ref.description: Where in the FFCA paper this rule is justified (e.g., 'Table 5 row 6', 'App C.6 Fig 18').

applies_when.description: Optional gate. The rule fires only when the expression holds against the attached CaseMeta. Supported grammar: case.<attr> == '<value>', case.<attr> != '<value>', case.<attr> in ('a', 'b'), case.<attr> not_in ('a', 'b'). Example: applies_when: "case.checkpoint_kind == 'epoch'". Un-parseable expressions raise at evaluation time.

signal.description: Dotted path: feature.impact | feature.volatility | feature.nonlinearity | feature.interaction | feature.archetype | model.archetype_dist.<archetype_snake> | model.impact_mean | training.val_score_curve | training.volatility_curve | trust.<decision>.count | cosens.best_nc_fraction | etc.

value.description: Scalar, list, or expression string. For time-series ops, may also be an object: {when, threshold_ratio, baseline_fraction, window_fraction} for spike_detected; {relative_improvement, tail_fraction} for plateau_detected; {final_threshold, relative_to} for collapse_detected.
